jobs.title: Blackstone Private Wealth - Client Services, Analyst
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients as well as providing ongoing education, training, and support around alternatives.

Specific responsibilities include:  

  • Field operational inquiries from Financial Advisors & internal sales teams on Blackstone investment offerings.

  • Liaise with Blackstone business units, transfer agents, and other third-party service providers relating to client inquiries, problem resolutions, and reconciliations.

  • Project management across various groups within Blackstone Private Wealth to enhance collaboration and efficiencies.

  • Identify and document operational procedures for ongoing fund maintenance on various platforms.

  • Develop strong understanding of distribution partners’ operational platforms and processes and become resident Blackstone expert for advisors.

  • Assist Financial Advisors with problem resolution of account opening, maintenance items and other inquiries related to client onboarding, servicing and client retention (deadlines, platform questions, redemptions, capital calls, distributions).

  • Assist in ad hoc projects / research to support Blackstone Private Wealth business initiatives.

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.  The Investor Services Associate should exhibit strong technical and analytical skills. Candidates should be driven, detailed and team-oriented with innovative ideas and solutions who thrive on challenges in a dynamic environment.

In addition, the successful candidate must meet the following qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • 2+ years of financial industry experience

  • Series 7 and Series 63, preferred

  • Strong Excel skills

  • Strong verbal and written communication skills

  • Attention to detail and accuracy

  • Strong problem analysis and resolution skills

  • Identify and summarize issues and recommendations

  • Work efficiently in a deadline driven environment

  • Manage multiple projects simultaneously

  • Work independently in a team‐oriented environment

  • Adaptable and collaborative

  • Strong understanding of the high net worth and Financial Advisor operating model

  • Knowledge of alternative investment funds and operational terms is helpful

  • Develop strong working relationships with colleagues, teams, and clients


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$100,000 - $130,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. Certain employees may also be eligible for equity and other incentive compensation at Blackstone’s sole discretion.

Blackstone is committed to providing equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, disability, genetic predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other class or status in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including but not limited to hiring, placement, promotion, termination, transfer, leave of absence, compensation, and training.  All Blackstone employees, including but not limited to recruiting personnel and hiring managers, are required to abide by this policy.

If you need a reasonable accommodation to complete your application, please contact Human Resources at 212-583-5000 (US), +44 (0)20 7451 4000 (EMEA) or +852 3656 8600 (APAC).

Depending on the position, you may be required to obtain certain securities licenses if you are in a client facing role and/or if you are engaged in the following:

  • Attending client meetings where you are discussing Blackstone products and/or and client questions;

  • Marketing Blackstone funds to new or existing clients;

  • Supervising or training securities licensed employees;

  • Structuring or creating Blackstone funds/products; and

  • Advising on marketing plans prepared by a sales team or developing and/or contributing information for marketing materials.

Note: The above list is not the exhaustive list of activities requiring securities licenses and there may be roles that require review on a case-by-case basis.  Please speak with your Blackstone Recruiting contact with any questions.

To submit your application please complete the form below. Fields marked with a red asterisk * must be completed to be considered for employment (although some can be answered "prefer not to say"). Failure to provide this information may compromise the follow-up of your application. When you have finished click Submit at the bottom of this form.

jobs.title: Private Equity Investor Product Strategy, AVP
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Job Description:       

The Investor Product Strategy team is responsible for the preparation of reporting materials and supports the marketing and fundraising teams’ efforts related to ongoing LP relationship maintenance with respect to each business’ funds.

The role focuses on financial analysis, performance-related data and investor reporting to support the marketing and content needs for prospective and current investors in the funds within the Private Equity business.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Prepare and analyze detailed investment reports ensuring accurate and timely delivery of financial insights to investors and collaborating with other internal finance teams to provide data driven support and content for investor relations activities
  • Assist with the preparation and review of marketing and fundraising materials including investor event presentations, conference/webinar materials, pitch books, talking points, performance memos, and other ad-hoc analyses
  • Coordinate with the fundraising team, deal team, and other finance and functional groups to collate and review investor/distributor due diligence requests and assist with investor queries
  • Coordinate the collection and analysis of performance metrics of the funds and underlying companies and other portfolio attributes across multiple internal teams and assist in preparation and review of bespoke fund performance analyses
  • Collaborate with the fundraising and marketing teams, deal teams, finance teams, and legal and compliance teams in supporting product development, existing clients and prospective investors
  • Provide mentorship and guidance to Analysts and Associates, including assisting with overseeing their day-to-day responsibilities and providing ongoing guidance to ensure alignment with team goals and timelines

Qualifications:                                   

  • Undergraduate degree in Finance, Marketing, Accounting, Economics, or a quantitative discipline;
  • Strong record of academic achievement and/or professional accomplishment
  • 6+ years of experience in a reporting related role in finance, investor reporting, transaction services, investment operations, consulting, or other related experience
  • Experience managing a team of at least 2 individuals is a plus
  • Strong attention to detail
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Outstanding organizational skills
  • Strong analytical and quantitative experience
  • Strong work ethic; works effectively in a dynamic team environment and handles multiple projects
  • Highly proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Highly motivated and results oriented  


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

$130,000 - $200,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. jobs.title: Blackstone Private Wealth - Product Specialist, Vice President (Real Assets)
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capabilities to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternative investments.


We leverage the global scale platforms across Private Equity, Secondaries, Real Estate, Infrastructure, Credit, Tactical Opportunities and Hedge Fund Solutions to structure single asset class and multi-asset solutions to fit into clients’ asset allocation and portfolio construction objectives. Blackstone manages $250 billion of capital from Private Wealth clients. Blackstone Private Wealth is globally positioned, with offices in New York, London, Zurich, Paris, Singapore, Hong Kong and Tokyo and works with many of the world’s largest wealth management institutions, independent wealth managers and family offices.

Product Specialists in Private Wealth play an integral role in providing investment detail to client stakeholders.  They cover the entire product lifecycle, from fundraising through investment and return-of-capital.  They provide domain knowledge and expertise on investments, performance, market dynamics and competitors.  Product Specialists have key internal and external stakeholders, including BX investment teams, Private Wealth sales professionals, various due diligence teams, financial advisors, and end clients.
 

The Product Specialist will focus on Blackstone’s Private Real Estate and Private Infrastructure strategies and products. This includes direct coverage of relevant strategies and providing strategic support to Private Wealth stakeholders executing sales initiatives across global channels. The VP will also oversee junior team members supporting the same functions.
 

Primary job responsibilities will include:

  • Partner with Real Estate and Infrastructure investment teams and internal stakeholders to develop and execute sales strategies across products and client segments — including content creation, messaging, competitive analysis, and product positioning:
  • Provide ongoing key real-time product, investment and market updates to the sales teams, advisors, end-clients, portfolio managers and other key stakeholders.
  • Develop and maintain high-impact content tailored to Private Wealth channels and business units – including monthly updates and commentary, quarterly fund reviews, investment themes, investment activity and deal updates, and various ad-hoc client requests.
  • Address client and advisor inquiries related to private real estate and private infrastructure products and the underlying markets in which these strategies invest.
  • Attend sales meetings with existing and prospective advisors and end clients in order to communicate portfolio and performance updates as well as market commentary.
  • Work across Private Wealth business functions in supporting product initiatives such as Marketing, Sales, Investor Services, Product Strategy and Business Development
  • Research competitive landscapes across alternative asset classes, strategy types and product structures.
  • Travel for in-person client engagement as needed.
     

Qualifications:

In addition to a minimum of 7+ years of experience in investment banking, asset management, consulting, or wealth management, with a focus on real assets (private real estate and/or private infrastructure), as well as strong interpersonal, analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills, the successful candidates must meet the following qualifications:

  • Deep understanding of private real estate and private infrastructure markets, investment structures, and performance drivers.
  • Desire to work in a collaborative, team-based environment.
  • Creative and articulate; effectively understands and conveys complex concepts
  • Demonstrable effective writing skills
  • Strong communication and presentation skills.
  • Familiarity with markets, securities, investment strategies and the relevant regulatory framework.
  • MBA and/or CFA preferred but not required.


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$175,000 - $200,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. Certain employees may also be eligible for equity and other incentive compensation at Blackstone’s sole discretion.

jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Blackstone is seeking a highly motivated professional to support our Private Wealth Business Development team in expanding our financial planning, analytics, and reporting capabilities. This role will have a variety of responsibilities and will work in close coordination across PW & Blackstone teams to:

  • Generate actionable business insights through in-depth analysis of key business initiatives
  • Prepare and deliver presentations to senior management, summarizing findings, recommendations, and potential impacts
  • Produce routine financial reporting to track, plan and analyze Private Wealth group expenses
  • Assist in global strategic planning, annual budget and business management routines
  • Model financial impact of business initiatives and new product structures
  • Create measurement frameworks for global initiatives with relevant KPIs
  • Drive best practices around data management, including proactive efforts to ensure data integrity and quality control
  • Support management of datasets and tools to enable efficient, effective and accurate reporting
  • Consolidate, organize, and manage information from numerous internal and external sources

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.

The successful candidate must have:

  • 3+ years of experience in financial services, wealth management, asset management or other related business experience – prior Financial Planning & Analysis experience preferred
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Expert in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Knowledge and proficiency across Tableau, Salesforce, Sigma
  • Strong academic record
  • Strong analytical, quantitative, and problem-solving skills
  • Highly motivated and driven; works effectively in a team environment
  • Strong work ethic, attention to detail and multi-tasking skills
  • Entrepreneurial, operates independently once adequately trained

jobs.title: Private Wealth - Sales & Promotions, Analyst/Associate
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Firm Overview:

Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. Blackstone seeks to deliver compelling returns for institutional and individual investors by strengthening the companies in which the firm invests. Blackstone’s $1.3 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , , and . 

Business Unit:

Blackstone Private Wealth  

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives.

Job Title:

Sales & Promotion, Analyst/Associate

Job Description:

The role will be involved in building relationships and maintaining contacts with distributors and asset management companies (sub-advisory partners), with the goal of accelerating the adoption of Blackstone investment solutions and growing the private wealth business in Japan.

The role will be responsible for understanding and articulating Blackstone’s sophisticated products (fund objectives, terms, exposures, characteristics, positioning, performance, etc.), providing promotion activities by conducting teach-in sessions and end-client seminars for distributor (at branches or via online) to support persistent growth of clients’ assets through the product lifecycle and maintaining client services by responding to inbound inquiries and working with professionals across Blackstone (including investments, legal/compliance department, finance, and other groups within the firm) to resolve clients’ issues.

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. The successful candidate must have: 

  • Bachelor’s degree required

  • ~9 years of professional working experience in financial services industry (asset management, wealth management or alternatives industry preferred, but not mandatory)

  • Great presentation skills (externally for distributors and end-clients, internally involving senior management)

  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in both Japanese and English

  • Demonstrate passion for capital markets, alternative investments, and retail and high-net worth businesses in Japan

  • Ability to perform at a high-level within a dynamic, entrepreneurial culture with a focus on excellence

  • Relevant Local Financial Product Sales Licenses (if not, need to obtain after joining organization)

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office including PowerPoint, Word, Excel and Outlook

Blackstone Private Wealth  

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives.

Job Title:

Client Coverage, Analyst/Associate

Job Description:

The role will support building relationships and maintain a high level of contact with distributors such as large financial institutions and global private banks as well as asset management companies in Japan, with the goal of accelerating the adoption of Blackstone investment solutions.

The role will also be the subject matter expert across one of the most diverse sets of alternative investment offerings in the industry by having significant interface with members of the firm’s global Private Wealth group.

In addition to gaining substantial technical knowledge on alternative investments, the role will also develop expertise across other important aspects including legal documentation, product development, operations, marketing, strategic planning and more.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Maintain a high-level of client service by responding to inbound inquiries and working with colleagues across Blackstone and Private Wealth (including investments, legal/compliance department, finance, and other groups within the firm) to resolve clients’ issues as an internal sales.

  • Provide strong support to the full fundraising cycle end-to-end by working closely with global and local colleagues across different functions, from client onboarding, product launches and post-launch services.

  • Demonstrated ability to understand and articulate Blackstone’s sophisticated products. (fund objectives, terms, exposures, characteristics, positioning, performance, etc.)

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. The successful candidate must have: 

  • Bachelor’s degree required

  • Professional working experience (minimum 3 years’ experience for Associate level) in financial services industry (asset management, wealth management, or alternatives industry preferred, but not mandatory)

  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in both Japanese and English

  • Demonstrate passion for capital markets, alternative investments, and retail and high-net worth businesses in Japan

  • Ability to perform at a high-level within a dynamic, entrepreneurial culture with a focus on excellence

  • Relevant Local Financial Product Sales Licenses (if not, need to obtain after joining organization)

We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Multi-Asset Investing (BXMA) manages $93 billion across a diversified set of businesses. We strive to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns for our clients across market cycles. Our strategies include Absolute Return, Multi-Strategy, Total Portfolio Management, and Public Real Assets.

Job Description:

The Portfolio Specialist will sit within the Portfolio Strategy Group (PSG) and serve as the primary point of contact for a group of clients with customized absolute return portfolios.

PSG is an integral part of the Absolute Return investment team and works closely with all BXMA’s strategy-focused hedge fund research teams and the Portfolio Management team.

The role primarily will focus on conducting recurring client meetings to discuss portfolio performance and attribution, opportunity set, and client objectives.  The Portfolio Specialist will liaise with BXMA’s Institutional Client Solutions team, and a variety of other teams within BXMA, to ensure that:

  • Portfolios are positioned to accomplish the long-term objectives of our clients.

  • Performance outcomes are well understood by our clients in the context of broader market and industry developments.

  • Clients view BXMA as a long-term partner that can help enhance their organizations beyond simply managing their capital.        

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.  In addition to strong analytical and time management skills, the successful candidate must meet the following qualifications:

  • 7+ years of hedge fund industry experience, preferably as an allocator or direct investor

  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal

  • Advanced Excel skills and effectively works with large amounts of performance and risk data

  • A desire to work in a dynamic environment

  • Manages ad hoc projects independently and to completion

  • A passion for global financial markets and hedge funds, and an understanding of different strategies that hedge funds employ

jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Private Capital Solutions)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill (“Park Hill”) is our industry-leading advisor and intermediary business focused on alternative investment vehicles in the private equity, real estate, hedge fund and secondary advisory asset classes.

The Private Capital Solutions group, our secondary advisory practice, is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions since the team’s founding in 2008. The practice leverages the firm’s global relationships to deliver superior results across the secondaries universe. Our highly specialized team focuses on delivering independent advisory and execution services to financial sponsors and institutional investors across a broad spectrum of strategic transactions including:

  • Financial Sponsor Solutions: GP-led continuation vehicles, fund restructurings, fund tender offers, preferred equity / other debt-oriented solutions, management team spinouts and portfolio asset sales;
  • Structured Liquidity Solutions: Securitizations, refinancings of illiquid financial holdings and joint ventures; 
  • Institutional Investor Solutions: Divesture of private fund interests, valuation of private fund interests, transaction structuring and execution support.

Analysts within the Private Capital Solutions group work directly with clients in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives. The remit of the position spans the private capital spectrum with an emphasis on private equity secondary transactions. The role is industry and asset class agnostic, and the Analyst will be a generalist across transactions.

Analysts are key deal team members who are staffed directly on client teams and are expected to be involved in all aspects of a transaction, including due diligence, valuation analysis, client and process management, and investor engagement.

The comparatively small size of this group and the frequent cross-collaboration across PJT offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members across the PJT platform.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s degree required; Finance or Economics major preferred;
  • At least 1 year of investment banking M&A, private equity or secondary advisory experience strongly preferred;
  • Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills;
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills;
  • Proven ability to work collaboratively and effectively within a team environment;
  • Exceptional organization and time management skills;
  • Strong work ethic and ability to work under pressure.

Expected annualized base salary of $120,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

Applicant Privacy Notice: View our Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Notice here. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Notice for further information.


*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.

  • Drafting of client presentations

  • Preparation of memoranda for internal and external use

jobs.title: Associate - Strategic Advisory (TMT)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Role Description

The Advisory group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years.  Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including, technology media telecommunications, natural resources, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, and real estate.

Associates within Advisory would be responsible for the following:

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures.

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate analyst materials.

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

  • Mentoring and training of juniors

The Associate will be a critical member of PJT’s expanding TMT team with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development, working on lean deal teams with direct exposure to the most senior practitioners. The role will span across a number of sectors. Given the lean nature of our teams all our bankers are flexible and should be prepared to work in sectors/countries outside their main area of focus. We are seeking candidates who possess knowledge of and interest in the Media and Software industry, as this role will be specifically within PJT’s TMT sector in Strategic Advisory.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Minimum Bachelor’s Degree

  • 2-5 years of relevant investment banking or corporate development experience

  • Experience with financial modelling including developing detailed operating and valuation models for complex transactions

  • Full breadth of financing (equity, equity linked and debt) and strategic advisory experience

  • Knowledge of and experience in the TMT industry, specifically media, technology and related transactions preferred

  • Resume must be in PDF format 

  • Fluency in English is required and in another European language preferred

  • Strong quantitative/analytical skills

  • Strategic and creative thinking; distinguished written and oral communications skills

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory Group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including, technology media telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, real estate gaming and leisure, and consumer retail.

The Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team is an extremely active and fast-growing group within PJT. The team is based across the New York and London offices with frequent collaboration across regions. The London team has been active on some of the most high-profile transactions in the market over the recent past, including for Aggreko, ASDA, ASOS, Arrow Global, Alstom, DIA, EG Group, EGYM, EIP, Job&Talent, and Porsche SE (amongst others).

As an Associate in the Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team, you will sit within the Advisory Group but also work frequently with the Restructuring and Special Situations Group who advise companies, financial sponsors and creditor groups in financially distressed situations.

  • You will work directly with financial sponsor and corporate clients across all industries in evaluating, structuring, recommending and executing financing alternatives across the capital structure

  • You will have the opportunity to advise clients on a broad range of transactions including (i) syndicated acquisition financings and refinancings, (ii) private credit lending, (iii) par and stressed refinancings, (iv) liability management, including A&Es and Exchange Offers, (v) growth and venture capital, and (vi) structured and infrastructure financings

  • The team sits at the fulcrum of PJT’s M&A and Restructuring franchises, thereby offering unparalleled exposure to clients and senior bankers, as well as exceptional deal-flow

  • Day-to-day responsibilities include analysing capital structures and cashflow models, credit documents and indentures; facilitating due diligence and discussions with companies, legal counsel and advisors; ratings analyses; and direct interaction with clients, banks and funds on capital raising transactions

  • Associates will be expected to manage analysts staffed on their transactions and on internal and external business development projects.

Qualifications

To be considered for the position, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in a top ranked university

  • 3-4 years of experience in leveraged finance, debt capital markets advisory, restructuring or direct lending at an investment bank or credit fund

  • ACA or CFA qualification preferred but not mandatory

  • Resume must include graduation month/year and GPA, if applicable

  • Resume must be in PDF format

jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory Group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including technology media telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, real estate gaming and leisure, and consumer retail.

The Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team is an extremely active and fast-growing group within PJT. The team is based across the New York, London and Dubai offices with frequent collaboration across regions. The London team has been active on some of the most high-profile transactions in the market over the recent past, and are trusted advisor to many of the most active issuers of sub-investment grade capital in the market. Recent transactions include those for: STADA (CapVest), Aggreko, ASDA, Arrow Global, EG Group, Applus (TDR), Energia, Ramudden (I Squared), DIA, EGYM, Job&Talent, and Porsche SE (amongst others).

The team advises clients on a broad range of transactions and structures, benefiting from being highly active in both public (High Yield, TLB), private markets (private credit, bank loans) and structured / hybrid products. We focus on deal structuring and negotiating with lenders on behalf of our clients. We have strong relationships with all top lenders, globally and run targeted, bespoke processes.

As a Vice President in the Strategic Capital Markets Advisory team, you will sit within the Strategic Advisory Group but will also work frequently with the Restructuring and Special Situations Group who advise companies, financial sponsors and creditor groups in financially distressed situations.

Responsibilities and expectations include:

  • Working directly with financial sponsor and corporate clients across all industries in evaluating, structuring, recommending and executing financing alternatives across the capital structure;

  • Advising clients on a broad range of transactions including (i) syndicated acquisition financings and refinancings, (ii) private credit lending, (iii) par and stressed refinancings, (iv) liability management, including Exit Financings, A&Es and Exchange Offers, (v) growth and venture capital, and (vi) structured / hybrid and infrastructure financings;

  • Collaborating directly with clients and working closely with senior members of the Strategic Advisory team, many of whom were previously group heads at leading investment banking firms;

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures;

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, financial analysis, and negotiation with lenders on key financing documents;

  • Working with senior management and coordinate associate / analyst materials;

  • Designing and developing financing models to assess the impact of various financing structures on companies and shareholders;

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings.

Qualifications

To be considered for the position, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in a top ranked university

  • Minimum 5 years of experience in leveraged finance, debt capital markets advisory, restructuring or direct lending at an investment bank or credit fund

  • ACA or CFA qualification preferred but not mandatory

  • Resume must include graduation month/year and GPA, if applicable

  • Resume must be in PDF format

Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory Group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including, technology media telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, industrials, healthcare, real estate gaming and leisure, and consumer retail.

The Capital Market Strategic Advisory team is seeking a Vice President with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to perform both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. As a Vice President you will sit within the Advisory Group but also work frequently with the Restructuring and Special Situations Group whom advises companies, financial sponsors and creditor groups in financially distressed situations.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate associate / analyst materials

  • Work directly with companies across all industries in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial alternatives across both the investment grade and non-investment grade ratings spectrum

  • Work on a wide variety of financing transactions including mergers, acquisitions, asset sales, restructurings and divestitures

  • The small size of this group offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members of the Strategic Advisory team, many of whom were previously group heads at leading investment banking firms

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Minimum Bachelor’s Degree

  • Minimum 6 years of investment banking experience

  • Multiple years of experience spent in debt infrastructure / infrastructure financing in an investment banking setting

  • A strong background in finance, accounting and legal documentation is required as you will be responsible for structuring and executing investment grade, leveraged, and distressed financings, including bonds, leveraged loans, equity-linked, and private capital solutions

  • Resume must be in PDF format  

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Private Equity - Project Management)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities:

PJT Park Hill’s Private Equity Group offers analysts the chance to engage directly in advising “best in class” private equity firms on all aspects of the capital raise – including strategic fundraising advisory, market positioning and ultimately raising capital from the top institutional investors across the globe.

The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across the financial spectrum.

Our Private Equity team is seeking a Project Management Analyst with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to work well both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. Each fundraising deal is typically run by a two-person deal team – a Senior Project Manager and an Analyst.

The Project Management Analyst will be based out of our London office and will provide support to PJT Park Hill’s business in Asia.

Candidates for the position must possess knowledge of and interest in the alternative asset industry—specifically private equity. The job requires management of multiple projects often under time constraints/deadlines, interaction and constant communication with clients, and great attention to detail.

Due to the nature of this role supporting the Hong Kong region, we ask for flexibility in the hours worked within this role, starting at 7am but finishing early. 

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Support the Hong Kong deal team in all aspects of the fundraising process;
  • Work directly with clients in drafting offering memorandums and marketing materials,
  • Coordinate roadshow scheduling and logistics;
  • Prepare for and coordinate all due diligence meetings and materials; complete LP due diligence questionnaires;
  • Assist the Partners in various research projects and the sourcing, diligence and origination of new mandates;
  • Manage and maintain external PJT Park Hill materials, including marketing/pitch materials, fund offering booklets and executive presentations for the senior Partners.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 1-2 years prior experience in private placements, investment banking, alternative asset investor relations or similar fields;
  • Bachelor Degree required with Accounting, Finance, or Economics major preferred
  • Strong, detail-oriented analytical skills;
  •  Knowledge of financial modelling and leveraged buyout analysis;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at a time
  • A self-starter with the ability to work effectively as part of a team
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Strong work ethic and results-oriented mentality;
  • Personal and professional integrity;
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Fluency in Japanese, Mandarin or Korean would be helpful

This role could offer future relocation opportunities to Hong Kong over the medium term (3-4 years) so would be particularly interesting to somebody connected to the region.

jobs.title: Principal - PJT Park Hill (Private Capital Solutions)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill (“Park Hill”) is our industry-leading advisor and intermediary business focused on alternative investment vehicles in the private equity, real estate, hedge fund and secondary advisory asset classes.

The Private Capital Solutions group, our secondary advisory practice, is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions since the team’s founding in 2008. The practice leverages the firm’s global relationships to deliver superior results across the private capital universe. Our highly specialized team focuses on delivering independent advisory and execution services to financial sponsors and institutional investors across a broad spectrum of strategic transactions including:

  • Institutional Investor Solutions: Divesture of limited partnership portfolios of private fund interests, valuation of private fund interests, transaction structuring and execution support.
  • Financial Sponsor Solutions: Fund recapitalizations, fund restructurings, tender offers, management team spinouts and portfolio asset sales;
  • Structured Liquidity Solutions: Preferred equity, NAV financing, Securitizations and other debt-oriented solutions for illiquid financial holdings

Principals within Private Capital Solutions would be responsible for the following:

  • Leading the advisory and execution of secondary transactions, including LP portfolio sales, GP-led solutions (continuation funds, restructurings, and tender offers) and other private market liquidity solutions;
  • Overseeing the structuring of complex transactions, including managing negotiations, pricing and term sheet development;
  • Overseeing financial modelling, valuation analysis and due diligence processes to ensure robust transaction execution;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements and due diligence sessions;
  • Managing and mentoring junior team members, providing guidance on deal execution and fostering their professional development.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 10+ years of financial services experience in private equity, secondary advisory or investment banking;
  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance is preferred but not required
  • Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills;
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills;
  • Proven ability to work collaboratively and effectively within a team environment;
  • Exceptional organization and time management skills;
  • Strong work ethic and ability to work under pressure.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

Applicant Privacy Notice:

View our Applicant Privacy Notice  info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Notice here. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Notice for further information.


*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.

Responsibilities

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory French Coverage team in Paris is seeking for an Associate with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to perform both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. The Advisory group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including technology, media & telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, oil & gas, industrials, healthcare, and real estate, gaming & lodging.

Associates within the Advisory group work directly with companies in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives. They typically work on a wide variety of transactions including mergers, acquisitions, joint ventures, asset sales, restructurings and divestitures. They are staffed directly on client teams and are expected to be involved in all aspects of a transaction, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements. The comparatively small size of this group offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members of the team, many of whom were previously group heads at leading investment banking firms.

Responsibilities for this individual will include:

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and develop quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and for review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • Minimum 3 years of directly comparable (Investment Banking) experience with particular focus on M&A

  • Fluency in French is required

  • Resume must be in PDF format

jobs.title: Vice President - Restructuring and Special Situations (French Speaker)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Restructuring and Special Situations Group advises large companies, governments and creditor groups in financially distressed situations. The group has been involved in most of the largest and most high-profile reorganizations that have occurred in the last decade. In a restructuring transaction, a wide range of strategic advisory services are provided, including advice on recapitalizations, reorganizations, corporate valuations, exchange offers, distressed mergers and acquisitions and raising debt and equity capital.

VPs in the Restructuring and Special Situations Group have the opportunity to develop a diverse and broad corporate finance skill set. Financial restructuring transactions are complex and involve multi-party negotiations with a number of key stakeholders including, among others, the board of directors, management, secured and unsecured lenders and equity holders.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Helping to lead the origination and execution of restructuring transactions across our platform;
  • Frequent senior-level interaction with clients and investors;
  • Developing detailed business plans;
  • Developing strategies to implement various restructuring transactions;
  • Evaluating financial returns to current and potential investors;
  • Attending key client negotiating and strategy sessions;
  • Managing complex and often time-sensitive restructuring transactions from start to finish;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 6 years of work experience in corporate concentrated in restructuring, leveraged & acquisition finance or credit
  • Fluency in English and French is required
  • Bachelor’s or Masters degree
  • Strong qualitative and analytical skills
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to run client meetings and coordinate deal execution
  • Strong written and presentation skills
  • A desire to work in a team environment, often under tight deadlines
  • Strong knowledge of accounting and finance
  • Ability to mentor and train more junior bankers
  • Personal and professional integrity

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Private Equity - Project Management)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill’s Private Equity Group offers analysts the chance to engage directly in advising “best in class” private equity firms on all aspects of the capital raise – including advisory work, market positioning and ultimately raising capital from the top institutional investors across the globe.

The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across the financial spectrum.

Our Private Equity team is seeking a Project Management Analyst with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to work well both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. Each fundraising deal is typically run by a two-person deal team – a Senior Project Manager and an Analyst.

Candidates for the position must possess knowledge of and interest in the alternative asset industry—specifically private equity. The job requires management of multiple projects often under time constraints/deadlines, interaction and constant communication with clients, and great attention to detail.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Support the deal team in all aspects of the fundraising process;
  • Work directly with clients in drafting offering memorandums and marketing materials,
  • Coordinate roadshow scheduling and logistics;
  • Prepare for and coordinate all due diligence meetings and materials; complete LP due diligence questionnaires;
  • Assist the Partners in various research projects and the sourcing, diligence and origination of new mandates;
  • Manage and maintain external PJT Park Hill materials, including marketing/pitch materials, fund offering booklets and executive presentations for the senior Partners.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 1-2 years prior experience in private placements, investment banking, alternative asset investor relations or similar fields;
  • Bachelor Degree required with Accounting, Finance, or Economics major preferred
  • Strong, detail-oriented analytical skills;
  •  Knowledge of financial modeling and leveraged buyout analysis;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at a time
  • A self-starter with the ability to work effectively as part of a team
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Strong work ethic and results-oriented mentality;
  • Personal and professional integrity;
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.

Expected annualized base salary of $120,000 to $130,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants.
